evidence_slots.subject: External JTAG/TAP requester or SimJTAG/SimDTM source driving DMI through dmi_jtag.
evidence_slots.operation: DMI read/write operations that can reach debug CSRs and issue downstream debug commands/SBA operations.
evidence_slots.object: dm_csrs registers/control outputs and downstream debug actions in dm_top.
evidence_slots.expected_guard: JTAG/DMI accesses that can issue debug module commands or system bus accesses should be gated by explicit authentication/authorization, e.g. jtag_key/pass_chk and/or debug enable/lifecycle condition, before dmi_req_valid_o reaches dm_csrs.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: dmi_jtag has jtag_key/pass/pass_chk signals, but the observed CDC instantiation forwards dmi_req_valid to core_dmi_valid_o without a visible pass_chk gate. dm_csrs sets dmstatus.authenticated = 1'b1 and accepts requests when dmi_req_valid_i & dmi_req_ready_o. pass is only loaded from jtag_key during reset; no observed evidence shows pass_chk mediating the outbound DMI valid path.
evidence_slots.path: dmi_jtag_tap TAP signals generate dmi_access/dmi_tdi/dmi_tdo; dmi_jtag constructs dmi_req and sends it through dmi_cdc to dmi_req_o/dmi_req_valid_o; dm_top forwards to dm_csrs; dm_csrs processes dmi_req_valid_i.
evidence_slots.state_condition: Reset loads pass <= jtag_key; DMI/TAP state resets on trst_ni/test_logic_reset, but authorization clearing or enforcement is not shown.
evidence_slots.impact: An external JTAG requester may be able to access debug CSRs, halt/resume harts, issue abstract commands, reset the system, or use debug SBA without proving possession of the JTAG key.

inspection_records.candidate_finding: DMI authentication appears incomplete: dm_csrs reports authenticated unconditionally and accepts DMI requests through the CDC path; the visible request-valid path into dm_csrs is not guarded by pass_chk/jtag_key.
inspection_records.notes: This is a permission finding based on explicit hardwired authentication and missing visible pass_chk gate on the DMI valid path.

evidence_slots.subject: DMI debug requester.
evidence_slots.operation: Write dmcontrol/command-like registers to halt, resume, select hart, and execute abstract command.
evidence_slots.object: haltreq_o, resumereq_o, cmd_valid_o, hartsel_o, debug_req_o path through dm_mem.
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Hart halt/resume and abstract command execution should require the selected hart to be valid/available and the debug module to be active/authorized; writes while cmdbusy should be rejected or recorded as errors.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: DMControl writes assign dmcontrol_d directly from DMI data. haltreq_o[selected_hart] and resumereq_o[selected_hart] are driven from dmcontrol_q bits. dmstatus reports nonexistent harts when hartsel exceeds NrHarts-1, but the output mux still indexes selected_hart. Command writes are gated by !cmdbusy_i and busy errors are recorded, satisfying part of the busy-state obligation but not auth/valid hart gating.
evidence_slots.path: DMI write in dm_csrs -> dmcontrol_q/command_q -> haltreq_o/resumereq_o/cmd_valid_o -> dm_top dm_mem -> debug_req_o.
evidence_slots.state_condition: cmdbusy_i protects command updates; unavailable_i and nonexistent are reflected in status but not observed gating haltreq/resumereq outputs.
evidence_slots.impact: An unauthenticated or improperly bounded DMI write could request hart halt/resume or command execution, potentially causing denial of service or arbitrary debug control of CPU execution.

inspection_records.candidate_finding: Hart control writes can drive haltreq/resumereq via selected_hart without a visible dmactive/auth guard or bounds guard on the output index; nonexistent status is reported but output generation still indexes selected_hart.
inspection_records.notes: Busy mediation is observed for command writes, but hart availability/bounds/auth gating is not observed at control-output generation.

evidence_slots.subject: Debug module SBA requester.
evidence_slots.operation: Read/write memory or peripherals through system bus access over AXI.
evidence_slots.object: System bus address space reachable via sbaddress/sbdata and axi_m_req_o.
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Debug system bus access should be constrained by debug authorization and by address/range/security-attribute checks appropriate for protected memory/peripheral regions; invalid sizes/addresses and busy updates should be blocked or signaled.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: dm_csrs exposes sbaddress, sbdata, sbaccess, sbreadonaddr/sbreadondata, and valid strobes. dm_top connects these directly to dm_sba, which drives axi_m_req_o. dm_sba receives dmactive_i but no visible address range or privilege filter is present in the inspected wrapper. Busy/error signaling exists, but writes to SBAddress/SBData update state even in blocks that set sbbusyerror.
evidence_slots.path: DMI write/read -> dm_csrs SBA registers -> dm_top i_dm_sba -> axi_m_req_o into system fabric/peripheral address map.
evidence_slots.state_condition: sbbusy_i can set sbbusyerror; sberror_i propagates to sbcs.sberror; dmactive_i is connected to dm_sba but range authorization is not visible.
evidence_slots.impact: A debugger with DMI access may be able to read or write protected memory and peripherals, including ROM2/key, AES, packet/PLIC, or RAM, bypassing CPU privilege/PMP-like checks.

inspection_records.candidate_finding: Debug SBA has a direct AXI master path controlled by DMI-written SBA CSRs, with no visible address/range/security-attribute mediation in the inspected connection-level RTL.
inspection_records.notes: dm_sba module body was not visible in observations; finding is based on wrapper-level absence of address filtering and the direct AXI master connection.

Some WARL masks are present for SATP/delegation/status fields.

inspection_records.notes: The observed code contains protected CSR update logic but not enough complete evidence to classify a violation or satisfaction.

Reset clears debug state. However, the observations do not show complete DRET decode/gating or debug CSR read/write decode for dpc/dscratch/dcsr.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Need complete DRET decode and debug CSR read/write cases to verify dret only in debug mode and dpc/dscratch access only under debug-mode/legal privilege.
inspection_records.notes: Upstream DMI authorization is questionable under PO-DBG-DMI-AUTH-001, but csr_regfile local lifecycle cannot be fully judged from provided snippets.

evidence_slots.subject: CPU CSR interface requester.
evidence_slots.operation: Configure address translation and privilege policy.
evidence_slots.object: SATP/status-derived translation controls and supervisor trap policy bits: satp_ppn_o, asid_o, en_translation_o, en_ld_st_translation_o, sum_o, mxr_o, tvm_o, tw_o, tsr_o.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Need full access-right predecode for supervisor/user writes to SATP/status and exception generation on illegal writes.
inspection_records.notes: Observed WARL/output behavior is partly satisfying, but access-control proof is incomplete.

evidence_slots.subject: AXI/APB bus requester, including CPU or debug SBA master.
evidence_slots.object: APB/AXI peripheral registers and data outputs, especially ROM2 key material, packet key table, AES, and PLIC.
evidence_slots.expected_guard: MMIO access to security-sensitive peripherals such as AES/key/packet/ROM2 should be mediated by address decode plus privilege/security attributes or explicit local guards; read data muxes should not expose secrets unless authorized.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: ariane_peripherals instantiates AXI/APB bridges that pass psel/pwrite/penable/paddr/pwdata/prdata to peripherals. ROM2 is connected via axi2mem with req/we/addr/wdata/rdata and key_reg_out feeds jtag_key/access_ctrl_reg. Packet and AES use apb_to_reg bridges. No observed wrapper signals check requester identity, AXI protection, privilege, or authorization before reaching these peripheral buses.
evidence_slots.path: System fabric AXI masters -> ariane_peripherals AXI/APB or AXI-to-memory bridges -> ROM2/PLIC/packet/AES register interfaces; debug SBA may reach the same fabric via axi_m_req_o.
evidence_slots.state_condition: Read/write enables derive from bridge handshakes; no observed unauthorized read default/error behavior beyond generic PSLVERR connections.
evidence_slots.impact: Any bus master with address reachability, including debug SBA, may read or overwrite key/ROM2 material or program security-sensitive packet/AES/PLIC registers without privilege mediation.

inspection_records.candidate_finding: Security-sensitive MMIO peripherals, including ROM2/key storage, packet, AES, and PLIC, are connected through generic AXI/APB bridges without visible requester privilege/security guards in the observed wrapper code.
inspection_records.notes: Peripheral module bodies for AES/packet were not fully visible; the candidate is based on visible top-level connectivity and absence of local guards in wrappers.

evidence_slots.subject: AXI/APB bus requester and debug authentication path.
evidence_slots.operation: Write/read ROM2 and route key material to JTAG authentication.
evidence_slots.object: ROM2 secure_reg contents, key_reg_out, jtag_key, rom2_rdata.
evidence_slots.expected_guard: ROM-like or key-location storage should reject writes or require a provisioning/privileged guard; any key or key-derived value routed to jtag_key or bus read data should have explicit authorization and safe reset semantics.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: rom2 initializes secure_reg from constant mem on reset, but when req_i and we_i are asserted it writes secure_reg[index] <= wdata_i. Reads set raddr_q and rdata_o returns secure_reg[raddr_q]. ariane_peripherals connects axi2mem we/addr/wdata to rom2 and assigns jtag_key = key_reg_out[1][31:0]. No write-protect, provisioning mode, or read authorization is visible.
evidence_slots.path: AXI access to rom2_fuse -> axi2mem i_axi2rom2 -> rom2 req/we/addr/wdata/rdata -> key_reg_out -> jtag_key/access_ctrl_reg.
evidence_slots.state_condition: Reset copies constants from mem to secure_reg; after reset writes are permitted on req_i && we_i; rdata_o reflects selected secure_reg entry.
evidence_slots.impact: A bus master can overwrite debug authentication key material and access-control registers, potentially enabling JTAG/DMI access or corrupting access-control policy; reads can expose secret key contents over the bus.

inspection_records.candidate_finding: ROM2 key/fuse storage is writable through the bus and readable through rdata_o, and key material is routed directly to jtag_key without a provisioning/write-protect or read-authorization guard.
inspection_records.notes: This obligation has direct RTL evidence of both writeability and readable key storage.

evidence_slots.subject: test/JTAG interface requester.
evidence_slots.operation: TAP shift/update/capture and DMI select under testmode_i.
evidence_slots.object: TAP instruction/data registers, dmi_access_o, dmi_tdi_o/dmi_tdo_i, tck clock path.
evidence_slots.expected_guard: testmode_i/DFT clock muxing should not create an unauthenticated path to shift/update DMI registers or force TAP state transitions that bypass jtag_key/pass_chk authorization.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: In the observed dmi_jtag_tap snippet, testmode_i only selects the DFT TCK clock mux between inverted and non-inverted TCK. TAP FSM shift/update/capture and DMI TDO mux are controlled by TAP state and instruction register, not directly by testmode_i. This does not satisfy overall DMI authentication, but no separate testmode-specific bypass was observed.
evidence_slots.path: testmode_i -> pulp_clock_mux2 selecting tck_n; TAP FSM remains driven by tms_i/tap_state_q; DMI access selected by DMIACCESS instruction.
evidence_slots.state_condition: trst_ni resets td_o/tdo_oe_o; testmode affects clocking edge path only in observed evidence.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: testmode_i trust/source boundary outside visible scope; downstream DMI auth issue remains covered by PO-DBG-DMI-AUTH-001.
inspection_records.notes: Classified as satisfied narrowly for testmode-specific bypass because testmode only affects clock muxing in observed snippets.

evidence_slots.subject: DMI debug requester.
evidence_slots.operation: Assert/deassert non-debug reset, activate/deactivate debug module, observe/reset status.
evidence_slots.object: ndmreset_o, dmactive_o, havereset_d, debug module internal command/SBA state.
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Non-debug-module reset and dmactive changes should be allowed only through authorized debug writes and should safely clear/retain state according to spec; reset requests must not be asserted by unauthenticated or stale DMI state.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: DMControl DMI writes assign dmcontrol_d directly from dmi_req_i.data, and dmactive_o is assigned from dmcontrol_q.dmactive. ndmreset_o is part of dm_csrs outputs and havereset is set when ndmreset_o is asserted. Given dmstatus.authenticated is hardwired and DMI request authentication is missing, reset controls are reachable by unauthenticated DMI writes. Observed snippets do not prove clearing of sbaddress/sbdata/command/progbuf when dmactive deasserts.
evidence_slots.path: JTAG/DMI -> dmi_jtag/dmi_cdc -> dm_top/dm_csrs -> DMControl write -> dmactive_o/ndmreset_o/havereset/debug state.
evidence_slots.state_condition: havereset_d is set on ndmreset_o and cleared by ackhavereset; reset domains include rst_ni and dmi_rst_ni but full sequential reset clearing not available in observations.
evidence_slots.impact: Unauthenticated or stale DMI state may reset the non-debug system, change debug module active state, or preserve command/SBA state across lifecycle transitions, causing denial of service or unauthorized post-reset debug actions.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Need full sequential block for dmcontrol_q/ndmreset_o and dmactive deassertion clearing of SBA/command/progbuf state.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: Debug reset/control registers can be written through the unauthenticated DMI path; dmactive/ndmreset and havereset behavior are visible, but authorization and complete safe clearing of command/SBA state on dmactive deassertion are not shown.
inspection_records.notes: Finding is primarily authorization/lifecycle-control exposure; complete state-retention subclaim remains partially uncertain.

31:     output logic        update_dr_o,
32:     output logic        capture_dr_o,
33: 
34:     // we want to access DMI register
35:     output logic        dmi_access_o,
36:     // JTAG is interested in writing the DTM CSR register
37:     output logic        dtmcs_select_o,
38:     // clear error state
39:     output logic        dmi_reset_o,
40:     input  logic [1:0]  dmi_error_i,
41:     // test data to submodule
42:     output logic        dmi_tdi_o,
43:     // test data in from submodule
44:     input  logic        dmi_tdo_i
45: 
46: );
47: 
48:     // to submodule
49:     assign dmi_tdi_o = td_i;
50: 
51:     enum logic [3:0] { TestLogicReset, RunTestIdle, SelectDrScan,
52:                      CaptureDr, ShiftDr, Exit1Dr, PauseDr, Exit2Dr,
53:                      UpdateDr, SelectIrScan, CaptureIr, ShiftIr,
54:                      Exit1Ir, PauseIr, Exit2Ir, UpdateIr } tap_state_q, tap_state_d;
55: 
56:     typedef enum logic [IrLength-1:0] {
57:         BYPASS0   = 'h0,
58:         IDCODE    = 'h1,
59:         DTMCSR    = 'h10,
60:         DMIACCESS = 'h11,
61:         BYPASS1   = 'h1f
62:     } ir_reg_t;
63: 
64:     typedef struct packed {
65:         logic [31:18] zero1;
66:         logic         dmihardreset;
67:         logic         dmireset;
68:         logic         zero0;
69:         logic [14:12] idle;
70:         logic [11:10] dmistat;
71:         logic [9:4]   abits;
72:         logic [3:0]   version;
73:     } dtmcs_t;
74: 
75:     // ----------------
76:     // IR logic
77:     // ----------------
78:     logic [IrLength-1:0]  jtag_ir_shift_d, jtag_ir_shift_q; // shift register
79:     ir_reg_t              jtag_ir_d, jtag_ir_q; // IR register -> this gets captured from shift register upon update_ir
80:     logic capture_ir, shift_ir, pause_ir, update_ir;
81: 
82:     always_comb begin
83:         jtag_ir_shift_d = jtag_ir_shift_q;
84:         jtag_ir_d       = jtag_ir_q;
85: 
86:         // IR shift register
87:         if (shift_ir) begin
88:             jtag_ir_shift_d = {td_i, jtag_ir_shift_q[IrLength-1:1]};
89:         end
90: 
91:         // capture IR register
92:         if (capture_ir) begin
93:             jtag_ir_shift_d =  'b0101;
94:         end
95: 
96:         // update IR register
97:         if (update_ir) begin
98:             jtag_ir_d = ir_reg_t'(jtag_ir_shift_q);
99:

5: module rom2 (

17: // Store key values here. // Replication of fuse. 
18:     const logic [RomSize-1:0][191:0] mem = {
19:         192'h00000000_00000000_00000000_00000000_0000f8f8_ff6fe00f, // 3rd location for Access control master 1. First 4 bits for peripheral 0, next 4 for p1 and so on.
20:         192'h00000000_00000000_00000000_00000000_0000fff8_ff6ff00f, // 2nd location for Access control master 0. First 4 bits for peripheral 0, next 4 for p1 and so on.
21:         192'h2b7e1516_28aed2a6_abf71588_09cf4f3c_2b7e1516_28aed2a6, // 1st location for JTAG
22:         192'h55555555_28aed2a6_abf71588_09cf4f3c_2b7e1516_28aed2a6  // oth location for AES
23:     };

25: // Secure registers. Key values copied from fuse to these registers on reset.
26: //    logic [RomSize-1:0][191:0] secure_reg;
27: 
28:     logic [$clog2(RomSize)-1:0] raddr_q;
29: 
30: // We can read and write initial 64 bits.
31: // On reset, key values will be copied to registers.
32:     always_ff @ (posedge clk_i) begin
33:         if (~rst_ni) begin
34:             secure_reg <= mem;
35:         end else begin
36:         if(req_i) begin
37:             if (!we_i) begin
38:                 raddr_q <= addr_i[$clog2(RomSize)-1+3:3];
39:             end else begin
40:                 secure_reg[addr_i[$clog2(RomSize)-1+3:3]] <= wdata_i;
41:             end
42:         end
43:         end
44:     end
45:     // this prevents spurious Xes from propagating into
46:     // the speculative fetch stage of the core
47:     assign rdata_o = (raddr_q < RomSize) ? secure_reg[raddr_q] : '0;
